description: 20-document corpus for default role BM25 regression gate

documents.title: Rust Programming Language
documents.body: Rust is a systems programming language focused on safety concurrency and performance. It prevents segfaults and guarantees thread safety.
documents.description: Official guide to the Rust programming language

documents.title: Knowledge Graph Systems
documents.body: Knowledge graphs represent entities and their relationships in a structured graph format. They enable semantic reasoning over connected data.
documents.description: Introduction to knowledge graph design and querying

documents.title: Search Engine Design
documents.body: Modern search engines use inverted indexes BM25 ranking and neural reranking to deliver relevant results to user queries.
documents.description: Architecture and algorithms behind web search engines

documents.title: Automata Theory Fundamentals
documents.body: Finite automata recognise regular languages. Pushdown automata handle context-free grammars. Turing machines compute all computable functions.
documents.description: Theoretical foundations of computation and automata

documents.title: Graph Algorithms and Data Structures
documents.body: Graph traversal algorithms include depth-first search and breadth-first search. Shortest path algorithms include Dijkstra and A-star.
documents.description: Algorithmic techniques for graph problems

documents.title: Async Rust with Tokio
documents.body: Tokio provides an asynchronous runtime for Rust. Async await syntax enables non-blocking IO operations without callback hell.
documents.description: Asynchronous programming patterns in Rust using Tokio

documents.title: Semantic Search with Embeddings
documents.body: Semantic search uses dense vector embeddings to find conceptually similar documents. Vector databases store and query these embeddings efficiently.
documents.description: Embedding-based semantic search systems

documents.title: Finite State Machines
documents.body: A finite state machine consists of states transitions and actions. Automata theory provides formal models for sequential logic systems.
documents.description: Design and implementation of finite state machines

documents.title: Rust Web Frameworks
documents.body: Axum Actix-web and Salvo are popular Rust web frameworks. They offer async request handling middleware and routing capabilities.
documents.description: Comparison of Rust web development frameworks

documents.title: Graph Database Systems
documents.body: Graph databases like Neo4j store data as nodes and edges. Property graphs support rich metadata on both nodes and relationships.
documents.description: Survey of graph database technologies

documents.title: Machine Learning Fundamentals
documents.body: Neural networks learn representations through backpropagation. Gradient descent optimises the loss function over training examples.
documents.description: Core concepts in machine learning and deep learning

documents.title: Distributed Systems Design
documents.body: The CAP theorem states distributed systems cannot simultaneously guarantee consistency availability and partition tolerance.
documents.description: Principles and patterns for distributed system architecture

documents.title: Python for Data Science
documents.body: Python libraries NumPy Pandas and Matplotlib are standard tools for data analysis visualisation and scientific computing.
documents.description: Python ecosystem for data science workflows

documents.title: Database Indexing Strategies
documents.body: B-tree indexes support range queries efficiently. Hash indexes excel at equality lookups. Covering indexes eliminate table scans.
documents.description: Indexing techniques for relational and NoSQL databases

documents.title: Compiler Design Principles
documents.body: Compilers consist of lexers parsers semantic analysers and code generators. LLVM provides a widely used compiler backend infrastructure.
documents.description: Phases and techniques in compiler construction

documents.title: Rust Memory Safety
documents.body: Rust ownership borrow checker and lifetimes enforce memory safety at compile time. No garbage collector means deterministic performance.
documents.description: How Rust achieves memory safety without runtime overhead

documents.title: Information Retrieval Methods
documents.body: Information retrieval systems rank documents by relevance to queries using BM25 TF-IDF and learned ranking models.
documents.description: Classic and modern information retrieval techniques

documents.title: Aho-Corasick String Matching
documents.body: The Aho-Corasick automaton enables simultaneous multi-pattern string matching in linear time. It is used in network intrusion detection and text search.
documents.description: Efficient multi-pattern string matching with automata

documents.title: Rust Error Handling
documents.body: Rust uses Result and Option types for explicit error handling. The question mark operator propagates errors ergonomically through call chains.
documents.description: Idiomatic error handling patterns in Rust

documents.title: Knowledge Representation Languages
documents.body: OWL RDF and SPARQL are W3C standards for knowledge representation and querying. They underpin the semantic web and linked data initiatives.
documents.description: Formal languages for knowledge representation
